2604.19395.title: Does Self-Consistency Improve the Recall of Encyclopedic Knowledge?
2604.19395.paper_summary: While self-consistency is known to improve performance on symbolic reasoning, its effect on the recall of encyclopedic knowledge is unclear due to a lack of targeted evaluation grounds. To address this, we establish such a knowledge recall split for the popular MMLU benchmark by applying a data-driven heuristic from prior work. We validate this split by showing that the performance patterns on the symbolic reasoning and knowledge recall subsets mirror those of GSM8K and MedMCQA, respectively. Using this solid ground, we find that self-consistency consistently improves performance across both symbolic reasoning and knowledge recall, even though its underlying CoT prompting is primarily effective for symbolic reasoning. As a result, we achieve an 89\% accuracy on MMLU, the best performance to date with the use of GPT-4o.
2604.19395.paper_summary_zh: 雖然自我一致性被認為能改善符號推理的表現，但由於缺乏針對性的評估基礎，其對百科知識回憶的影響尚不明確。為了解決這個問題，我們通過應用先前工作的數據驅動啟發式方法，為流行的 MMLU 基準建立了這樣的知識回憶劃分。我們通過顯示符號推理和知識回憶子集的表現模式分別與 GSM8K 和 MedMCQA 的模式相似來驗證這一劃分。在這一堅實的基礎上，我們發現自我一致性在符號推理和知識回憶兩方面的表現上都能持續改善，即使其底層的 CoT 提示主要對符號推理有效。因此，我們在 MMLU 上達到了 89\% 的準確率，這是迄今為止使用 GPT-4o 的最佳表現。
2604.19395.author: Sho Hoshino et.al.
2604.19395.authors: Sho Hoshino, Ukyo Honda, Peinan Zhang
